decks.notes: The main concept of this deck is to quickly and aggressively disrupt the opponent's hand and board state using cards like Duress, Cabal Therapy, and Hypnotic Specter, while also applying pressure with efficient creatures like Ravenous Rats and Withered Wretch. The deck aims to control the game through constant hand disruption while chipping away at the opponent's life total, ultimately overwhelming them with a combination of card advantage from Phyrexian Arena and powerful creatures like Masticore. The deck also includes removal spells like Smother and Contagion to deal with opposing threats.

The main goal of this deck is to quickly get out Phyrexian Dreadnought and use Stifle to counter its ability, giving you a powerful creature for a low cost. The deck also includes various card draw and counter spells to control the game until you can achieve this combo. Powder Keg and Portent provide additional utility and control options. Overall, this deck aims to disrupt your opponent's plans while quickly assembling a game-winning combo with Phyrexian Dreadnought.

This deck focuses on controlling the opponent's resources while utilizing Oath of Druids to quickly put powerful creatures into play. By disrupting the opponent's mana base with cards like Wasteland and Rishadan Port, and slowing down their spells with Sphere of Resistance, the deck aims to establish a superior board presence and win the game with efficient creatures like Terravore. The inclusion of various land destruction spells and interactive cards like Swords to Plowshares provide additional control elements to maintain dominance throughout the game.

The deck relies on a combination of aggressive creatures and land destruction spells to disrupt the opponent's game plan while maintaining pressure on the board. By utilizing cards like Terravore, Armageddon, and Weathered Wayfarer, the deck aims to control the battlefield and pave the way for consistent attacks. Meddling Mage and Sylvan Safekeeper provide additional utility and protection, while cards like Swords to Plowshares and Naturalize help deal with problematic threats. The deck's mana base is optimized for efficiency and consistency, allowing for effective plays and resource management throughout the game.

This deck focuses on overwhelming the opponent with a swarm of Elves, utilizing cards like Priest of Titania and Wirewood Symbiote to generate mana and reuse creatures for maximum efficiency. The deck also includes powerful finishers like Biorhythm and Masticore to quickly close out games. Survival of the Fittest is a key card for tutoring up essential creatures, while Tangle Wire provides disruption to control the pace of the game. The deck aims to quickly build a board presence and outpace the opponent with its aggressive Elf tribal strategy.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly assemble the combo of Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to create a powerful threat while denying the opponent's answers. The deck utilizes various counterspells and card draw spells to protect the combo and find the necessary pieces. It aims to control the game by disrupting the opponent's plays and efficiently winning the game through the stiflenought combo. Additionally, it includes some utility cards like Powder Keg and Mishra's Factory to provide additional options and versatility in gameplay.

The goal of this deck is to quickly assemble the combo of Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle, allowing for a powerful creature to enter the battlefield without its downside being activated. The deck also includes a control package with Counterspell and other counterspells to protect the combo, as well as card draw and filtering to find the necessary pieces. The inclusion of Mishra's Factory provides an alternate win condition if the combo is disrupted.

The deck focuses on using aggressive creatures like Nimble Mongoose and Terravore to quickly pressure the opponent while disrupting their game plan with cards like Meddling Mage, Swords to Plowshares, and Mox Diamond. The key strategy is to quickly ramp up mana with Weathered Wayfarer and Gemstone Mine to cast powerful spells like Armageddon and Terrageddon, utilizing lands like Wasteland and Gaea's Cradle to maintain board control. The deck also includes card draw with Sylvan Library to ensure consistent threats and answers are available throughout the game.

This deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana production with various Elf creatures to power out big threats like Deranged Hermit and Kamahl, Fist of Krosa. It also includes cards like Survival of the Fittest to search for key creatures and Tangle Wire to slow down the opponent. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with an aggressive board presence while also having some utility and removal options to control the game. Additionally, the inclusion of Gaea's Cradle allows for explosive turns when multiple creatures are in play.

This deck focuses on deploying a horde of aggressive Goblin creatures quickly, leveraging cards like Goblin Lackey and Goblin Warchief to overwhelm the opponent. With a mixture of efficient and powerful creatures along with some disruption in the form of Gempalm Incinerator and Wasteland, the deck aims to maintain pressure and close out the game swiftly. The inclusion of utility cards like Goblin Matron and Siege-Gang Commander also provides additional options for adapting to different game situations.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with enchantments using cards like Attunement and Frantic Search, then using Replenish to return them all to play for a powerful combo finish. Opalescence turns enchantments into creatures, while Parallax Tide and Parallax Wave provide disruption and removal. Mana Leak and Stifle help control the game while Swords to Plowshares offers removal. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a board full of enchantments and close out the game quickly.

The deck focuses on utilizing the Replenish card to bring back enchantments from the graveyard, specifically Opalescence and Parallax Wave. This creates a powerful combo where Parallax Wave can exile opposing creatures indefinitely. The deck also includes cards like Tsabo's Web and Mana Leak to control the opponent's board while setting up the combo. The deck's mana base is designed to ensure consistent access to white and blue mana for casting spells and activating abilities. Overall, the strategy revolves around assembling the combo pieces to dominate the game.

The main focus of this deck is to create a lock-down situation for the opponent by using Stasis, Forsaken City, and Root Maze to prevent them from untapping their permanents. To support this strategy, there are also counterspells like Counterspell and Arcane Denial, along with card draw options like Gush and Impulse. Other cards like Black Vise and Powder Keg can put pressure on the opponent while managing resources. The deck aims to stall the game until it can assemble the combo pieces needed to secure victory.

The main focus of this deck is to strategically play Phyrexian Dreadnought in combination with Stifle to create a powerful combo. The deck also includes various counterspells and draw spells to efficiently control the game and protect the combo. Additionally, cards like Vision Charm and Chain of Vapor provide additional utility and flexibility. With a streamlined approach to achieving its win condition, this deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's strategy in a premodern 1 vs 1 format.

This deck aims to quickly assemble a powerful combo involving Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle, preventing opponents from countering or removing the Dreadnought. Winter Orb is used to slow down opponent's mana development, while the deck includes a suite of counterspells and card draw to protect and find the combo pieces. The key strategy is to disrupt opponents while setting up the game-winning combo as fast as possible, relying on efficient plays and resource management to outmaneuver opponents in a premodern 1 vs 1 format.

The deck aims to quickly assemble the Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle combo, which allows for a powerful creature to enter the battlefield without its drawback. The deck includes a range of counter and disruption spells to protect the combo and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The focus is on efficiently drawing through the deck to find the necessary pieces while maintaining control of the game through counter magic and bounce spells.

This deck aims to delay the opponent's strategy and control the game through counterspells, bounce spells, and card advantage. It utilizes land disruption with Powder Keg and Dust Bowl, while also having ways to generate card advantage with Accumulated Knowledge and Fact or Fiction. The deck utilizes a mix of creature lands and utility lands to provide alternate win conditions and ways to interact with the opponent's creatures. Overall, the deck focuses on maintaining control of the game and disrupting the opponent's game plan.

The deck focuses on quickly generating mana through elves to cast powerful creatures and spells. It also includes cards that allow for creature recursion and protection, as well as disruption in the form of Tangle Wire. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with its fast mana production and aggressive creatures, while also having ways to recover if the opponent manages to stabilize. Ultimately, the goal is to establish board dominance and pressure the opponent into submission before they can mount a defense.

The deck focuses on using Enchantress creatures to draw additional cards every time an enchantment is cast. By combining cards like Sterling Grove, Enchantress's Presence, and Mirri's Guile with powerful enchantments like Opalescence and Parallax Wave, the deck can quickly overwhelm opponents with a powerful board state. The deck also includes elements of control with cards like Swords to Plowshares and Seal of Cleansing, while utilizing cards like Wild Growth and Serra's Sanctum to ramp up mana quickly. The overall strategy is to establish a strong enchantment presence while controlling the board and overwhelming opponents with a combo finish.

The main idea of this deck is to use cards like Survival of the Fittest, Intuition, and Cabal Therapy to set up a combo involving cards like Phyrexian Devourer, Triskelion, and Hypnox. The deck aims to efficiently search for and play these combo pieces while disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Duress and Swords to Plowshares. The mana base includes a variety of lands to help fix mana and accommodate the multicolored spells in the deck. It's a complex Combo archetype that requires careful planning and strategic thinking to execute effectively.

The deck focuses on assembling a combo involving graveyard manipulation with Hermit Druid and recursion with cards like Unearth and Survival of the Fittest. It includes various creatures with strong abilities such as Volrath's Shapeshifter and Akroma, Angel of Wrath to support the combo. The deck also includes disruption elements like Cabal Therapy and Duress to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Overall, the deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and graveyard synergy.

The deck is centered around quickly summoning and buffing up Goblin creatures to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive playstyle. It focuses on utilizing Goblin tribal synergies to generate card advantage and deal damage quickly. The inclusion of disruptive cards like Rishadan Port and Wasteland helps to control the opponent's mana base while the removal spells like Smother and Terminate deal with threats that may disrupt the Goblin swarm. Overall, the deck aims to rush down the opponent with a combination of creatures, burn spells, and disruption.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive goblin creatures and utilize their abilities to control the board. By using cards like Goblin Lackey to cheat out creatures, Goblin Piledriver to deal massive damage, and Gempalm Incinerator to clear the opponent's board, this deck aims to quickly establish board presence and maintain pressure. With cards like Goblin Matron to tutor for specific goblins and Goblin Ringleader for card advantage, the deck is built to consistently apply pressure while maintaining a level of control over the game.

The focus of this Mono Blue Control deck is to maintain control over the game by countering opponent's spells, drawing cards, and disrupting their strategies. It utilizes a combination of counterspells, card draw, and bounce effects to control the board and outpace the opponent. The deck also includes efficient win conditions like Phyrexian Dreadnought and Mishra's Factory to close out games once it has established control. The inclusion of utility lands like Dust Bowl and Faerie Conclave adds versatility and resilience to the deck's game plan.

The central focus of this Mono Blue Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's plans and maintain control over the game through countering spells and removal. This deck utilizes a mix of counterspells, card draw, and land destruction tactics to slow down the opponent's strategy while building up its own resources. The inclusion of powerful cards like Parallax Tide and Dust Bowl provides additional control options, while creatures like Phyrexian Dreadnought offer a win condition. Overall, the key strategy of this deck is to outlast the opponent and win through attrition.

The main focus of this deck is to control the opponent's resources and board through various spells and enchantments, while generating card advantage. The goal is to stall the game until the opponent is overwhelmed by a combination of discard spells, removal, and threats like Nether Spirit and Zombie Infestation. By utilizing efficient removal spells and disruptive elements like Duress and Contamination, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and eventually suffocate them with a steady stream of threats and disruption.

The deck focuses on minimizing opponent's resources and maximizing card advantage through cards like Winter Orb, Survival of the Fittest, and Opposition. It aims to control the game with creatures like Tradewind Rider and Wall of Blossoms, while using powerful lands like Gaea's Cradle to generate mana for its strategy. The deck is designed to slow down the opponent's tempo while building up its own resources, ultimately overwhelming the opponent with a strong board presence and efficient card advantage.

This deck focuses on using various creatures and enchantments to control the board and outvalue the opponent. The key strategy is to use Survival of the Fittest to tutor for key creature cards and set up powerful combos such as Spike Feeder with Phyrexian Devourer. Winter Orb and Opposition help to disrupt the opponent's resources and tempo, while creatures like Deranged Hermit and Tradewind Rider provide additional value and utility. The deck aims to outlast the opponent by controlling the battlefield and generating incremental advantage over time.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive playstyle, utilizing low-cost creatures and burn spells to deal damage quickly. The combination of creatures like Jackal Pup and Mogg Fanatic with powerful burn spells like Fireblast and Lightning Bolt allows for a relentless assault on the opponent's life total. The inclusion of cards like Cursed Scroll and Grim Lavamancer helps to maintain pressure on the opponent while controlling the board. With a strong emphasis on efficiency and speed, this deck aims to win the game as quickly as possible.

The focus of this deck is to quickly and efficiently bring out Phyrexian Dreadnought while utilizing various control and draw spells to maintain board presence and disrupt the opponent's strategy. By combining the "Stiflenought" combo with counterspells, card advantage, and stalling tactics, the deck aims to outmaneuver opponents and secure a victory through a powerful and unexpected win condition. It prioritizes consistency, resource management, and strategic timing to overwhelm the opponent and seize control of the game.

The main idea of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with fast and aggressive creatures while using pump spells and card draw to maintain pressure. The deck focuses on generating early mana with mana dorks like Fyndhorn Elves and Llanowar Elves, while also utilizing efficient threats like Call of the Herd and Wild Mongrel. Tangle Wire helps to slow down the opponent while Rancor provides a recurring source of damage. Overall, the deck aims to quickly establish a board presence and close out the game before the opponent has a chance to stabilize.

The main idea of this deck is to control the game by using cards like Goblin Welder, Tangle Wire, Winter Orb, Smokestack, and Sphere of Resistance to disrupt the opponent's strategy and slow down their plays. With the ability to tutor for powerful artifacts like Triskelion and Karn, Silver Golem using Tinker, the deck aims to quickly assemble a powerful board state and overwhelm the opponent. Additionally, cards like Meditate and City of Traitors provide additional mana acceleration to help cast these impactful spells.
